claim: The nullifier is computed from the raw coin data and the spending key, not from the commitment
The Zswap state historic roots are typed TimeFilterMap<MerkleTreeRoot> with time-based expiry
The native token type identifier is the 256-bit zero value
The native token type identifier is retrieved via nativeToken(): Bytes<32>
Custom token types are computed as Hash(contract_address, domain_separator)
The commitment tree is append-only: commitment_tree_first_free never decreases
The commitment tree history is typed TimeFilterMap<MerkleTreeRoot> and entries expire based on time window
Contract address is computed as Hash(contract_state, nonce), derived from the initial contract state and a nonce
MerkleTree state type stores only the root on-chain and its contents are private
Pedersen commitments are used separately for balance proofs, not as Merkle tree leaves
The entire UTXO is consumed on spending -- partial spends are impossible. Change is returned as a new UTXO
Midnight maintains a TimeFilterMap<MerkleTreeRoot> of valid past roots with time-based expiry
Midnight keeps the nullifier set append-only (does not prune spent outputs, unlike Bitcoin)
Bitcoin's Merkle tree contains transactions, while Midnight's Merkle tree contains coin commitments
